key_insight: A fixed-budget, cluster-aware judging protocol can dramatically change RAG benchmark conclusions, revealing that some apparent gains disappear under proper statistical handling.
engineering_relevance: For AI materials science workflows, this means retrieval and answer quality comparisons should be evaluated under strict budget, prompt, and clustered-data controls to avoid overstating system performance.
method: Fix the evaluation budget: top-100 candidate pool, evidence budget, answer cap, generator, and prompt.
Use pre-registered hypotheses plus cluster-aware inference, with an exact cluster sign-flip check when feasible.
Add second-judge replication to reduce judge-specific bias in LLM-as-a-judge scoring.
evidence_signal: On 400 multi-hop questions across CS/ML and Materials Science, a binomial test made all four semantic-baseline comparisons look significant, but cluster-aware inference reduced this to only one Bonferroni-significant result.
watch_item: Cluster-aware tests and second-judge replication add complexity, so results can depend strongly on whether the benchmark is sufficiently clustered and whether the exact sign-flip check is feasible.
paper_title: A Fixed-Budget, Cluster-Aware Standard for LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation: A Multi-Hop RAG Stress Test
